entries.statement: Knowledge of AI application security risks, including prompt injection, jailbreaking, data exfiltration via LLM outputs, and model supply chain vulnerabilities.
Knowledge of global data protection regulations, including GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, LGPD, PDPA, and sector-specific privacy obligations, and the data management requirements each creates.
Knowledge of privacy-by-design principles and how to embed privacy requirements into data platform design, data product development, and AI system design.
Knowledge of data subject rights management, including rights request intake, fulfillment workflows, response timelines, and documentation requirements.
Knowledge of data classification, sensitivity labeling, and the architectural controls required to enforce data access, privacy, and residency requirements by design.

Knowledge of the regulatory and compliance landscape governing enterprise data and AI, including data protection law, AI-specific regulation, sector-specific obligations, and cross-jurisdictional compliance requirements.

Knowledge of data protection law, including GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, and sector-specific privacy regulations, with emphasis on the legal bases for processing, data subject rights, cross-border transfer mechanisms, and enforcement precedent.

Knowledge of the Data Protection Officer's statutory role, responsibilities, and independence requirements under GDPR Articles 37-39, including reporting lines, conflict-of-interest prohibitions, and the distinction between advisory and decision-making authority.

Knowledge of Data Protection Impact Assessment methodology, including the conditions that make assessments mandatory, techniques for evaluating risk to individuals, and how to document residual risk and mitigation measures to supervisory authority standards.

Knowledge of privacy-by-design and privacy-by-default principles, including their application to system architecture, data minimization, purpose limitation, storage limitation, and consent management design patterns.
Knowledge of training data integrity risks, including data poisoning attack vectors, supply chain contamination, label manipulation, and the detection and prevention methods for each.

Knowledge of LLM-specific security vulnerabilities, including the OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications (prompt injection, insecure output handling, training data poisoning, model theft, excessive agency).
Knowledge of model supply chain security, including risks in open-source model adoption, fine-tuning pipeline integrity, third-party dataset provenance, and model registry security controls.

Knowledge of data protection regulatory requirements, including GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA, SOX data requirements, and sector-specific data regulations, and the specific data management obligations each creates.
